business_context.intent: Attribute POS transactions to BD-tracked business leads using business identity, warehouse territory, and Costco fiscal ordering.
business_context.warehouse_note: Warehouse values are runtime scope only. No warehouse number is a business rule.
business_context.attribution_note: No fixed 6-month, 180-day, rolling, or calendar-window attribution rule exists.

blocking_rules.new_match_fiscal_rule: A POS transaction is a new conversion only when it is on or after the lead fiscal point.
blocking_rules.new_match_expression: pos.fiscal_year > lead.fiscal_year OR (pos.fiscal_year = lead.fiscal_year AND pos.fiscal_period >= lead.fiscal_period)
blocking_rules.closed_existing_rule: A strong POS match before the lead fiscal point is Closed - Existing and is not credited as a new conversion.

matching_markers.email_phone_note: Agreement can boost confidence. Disagreement is neutral because POS email can be member-personal.

fuzzy_score_bands.name: Matching High
fuzzy_score_bands.label: Matching (High)
fuzzy_score_bands.business_meaning: Strong AI inference; still not Exact

semantic_definitions._purpose: Plain-English definitions of matching terms so LLM and human reviewers interpret results consistently. These definitions are authoritative for reporting and answering layers.
semantic_definitions.lead: A Business Development tracked business prospect record assembled from lead, account, and contact data. Business identity is based on business_name and full_address.
semantic_definitions.pos: A point-of-sale transaction record from the transaction table, representing actual purchase activity at a Costco warehouse.
semantic_definitions.warehouse_scope: The runtime warehouse set being processed. Matching is always blocked to the same warehouse_number; warehouse scope is not itself a scoring rule.
semantic_definitions.exact_match: A match produced only by the deterministic field-matching engine where normalized business identity fields agree. It is scored exactly 100, labeled Exact / Complete, and is authoritative.
semantic_definitions.deterministic_engine: The rule-based matching engine that runs first on the dataset. It claims exact matches before the semantic/fuzzy engine processes only residual unmatched records.
semantic_definitions.fuzzy_match: A match proposed by Vertex AI semantic embeddings using cosine similarity. Fuzzy matches are AI-inferred candidates scored 70-99.999 and are never labeled Exact.
semantic_definitions.matching_high: A fuzzy match scoring 90-99.999. It is treated as a high-confidence AI match with lifecycle_state Closed - Match, but remains AI-inferred and traceable as fuzzy-sourced.
semantic_definitions.potential_medium: A fuzzy match scoring 85-89.999 with lifecycle_state Potential. It is a likely candidate that needs business or ServiceNow confirmation.
semantic_definitions.potential_low: A fuzzy match scoring 70-84.999 with lifecycle_state Potential. It is a possible candidate that needs review.
semantic_definitions.no_match: Any computed fuzzy score below 70. It is rejected and no match row is written; scores are not clamped up to qualify.
semantic_definitions.manual_review: A match_type assigned when competing leads for the same POS are within the ambiguity_delta of each other. It routes to human review with lifecycle_state Potential and must never be treated as Exact.
semantic_definitions.combined_field: An embedding built from business_name plus full_address. It is used as the recall gate and candidate pre-filter only, not in the final weighted score.
semantic_definitions.recall_gate: The pre-filter step using combined_field cosine similarity and nearest-neighbor retrieval to choose which POS candidates are precision-scored.
semantic_definitions.precision_score: The final weighted fuzzy score for a candidate pair, computed from full_address_score and business_name_score only.
semantic_definitions.full_address_score: Cosine similarity from 0 to 100 between lead and POS full_address embeddings. It has weight 4, the strongest precision signal.
semantic_definitions.business_name_score: Cosine similarity from 0 to 100 between lead and POS business_name embeddings. It has weight 3.
semantic_definitions.final_score: The stored confidence score. Exact rows are 100. Fuzzy rows use the precision formula and are capped below 100 with max 99.999.
semantic_definitions.closed_match: lifecycle_state meaning the lead is treated as matched. It applies to deterministic Exact rows and Matching High fuzzy rows, but reports must split proven Exact from high-confidence AI.
semantic_definitions.closed_existing: lifecycle_state for strong POS activity before the lead fiscal point. It is pre-existing customer activity and is not credited as a new conversion.
semantic_definitions.potential: lifecycle_state for fuzzy 70-89.999 rows and Manual Review rows. It means a candidate awaiting confirmation, not a settled match.
semantic_definitions.no_match_state: Lifecycle outcome for scores below 70; no row is persisted.
semantic_definitions.primary_transaction: The earliest qualifying transaction per lead by fiscal_year, fiscal_period, and week among rows scoring at least 70.
semantic_definitions.ambiguity_delta: The score gap, 3 points, within which competing lead-to-POS claims are too close to auto-decide and are routed to Manual Review.
semantic_definitions.new_match_fiscal_rule: A POS transaction counts as a new conversion only when it is on or after the lead fiscal point. There is no 6-month, 180-day, rolling, or calendar-window attribution rule.
semantic_definitions.deterministic_boosts: Optional hard-identifier confidence additions: email exact match plus 5 and phone exact match plus 5, capped at 100. Disagreement is neutral.
semantic_definitions.authoritative_meaning: Authoritative means trusted enough to persist as a match. Exact is authoritative because it is rule-proven; Matching High is high-confidence AI but not deterministic proof.
semantic_definitions.exact_vs_fuzzy_summary: Deterministic exact matching runs first and owns score 100 / Exact / Complete. Vertex AI runs only on residual unmatched records and produces fuzzy candidates from 70 to 99.999 that are never relabeled Exact.

formula_explanation.denominator_reason: The denominator is 7 because it is the sum of the precision weights: full_address weight 4 plus business_name weight 3.
formula_explanation.combined_field_reason: combined_field is used as the recall gate to find candidate POS rows. It is not added again to the final score to avoid double-counting the same business identity signal.

primary_transaction.definition: Earliest qualifying transaction per lead by fiscal year, period, then week.
